series_title:
Certificates of
Convenience & Necessity
(CCN) - Central Records
series_description: Actual certificates issued.

Original maps prepared in conjunction
with the granting of the CCNs. Also
includes folded maps, slides, cassettes and
photographs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 35 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: After Retention
met and transferred to Archives,
will no longer be stored at the State
Records Center.

series_description:
Tariff schedules for all utilities, including
terms, conditions and rates of utilities'
services.

Computer database of complaints made
by citizen/consumers concerning utilities
regulated by the PUC.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Outage Report Information from
submitted by Service Providers to
document a significant interruption of
service.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Compliance audits and review performed
by the agency on utilities Real Electric
Provider (REP), Telecommunications
Service Provider (TSP), and Transmission
and Distribution Utility (TDU). Includes
associated workpapers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas State agencies.

series_description:
Workpapers of regulatory audits and
reviews performed by the agency on
utilities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Compliance audits and reviews performed
by the agency on vendors and contractors
receiving disbursements from the Texas
Universal Service Fund.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes audits and reviews performed by
the agency on internal operations (i.e.,
Internal Auditors Annual Report); audit
reports received from the State Auditor's
Office; the Texas Performance Review
Audit; also includes Internal Audit
workpapers, as required by Texas Internal
Auditor's Act.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains
any copies of it audit performed on
Texas State Agencies.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or
used to justify and support legislative
appropriations
requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the requests to
the Texas state publications
depository program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Only copies of supporting
documentation submitted to the
Legislative Budget Board are
archival.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming and outgoing
correspondence and internal
correspondence that are not transitory
and that pertain to or arise from, the
routine operations policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency. May be
in any medium.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007.

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a
regulation, policy, or procedure that
governs the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency.
comments:
NOTE: Agency policy to retain
longer than TSLAC minimum per
Executive office.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchase with state funds are
maintained by during business hours that
document appointments, itineraries and
other activities of agency officials or
employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchase with personal funds, but
use by a state official or employee
to document a work activities may
be record and subject to this
retention period. See Open Records
Decision 635 issued in December
1995 by the Atty. Gen.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General,
including any requests eliciting the
opinions.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinion or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic, audio,
and audiovisual records.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: A archival code
per Laura Saegert, Appraisal
Archivist 1996.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are exempted under the
Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Copies of all propose, withdrawn,
emergency and adopted rules; open
meeting notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of propose legislation and related
correspondence.

Surveys returned by the customers or
clients of an agency, and statistical data
maintained rating an agency's
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations or reports delivered in

comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: A archival code
per Laura Saegert, Appraisal
Archivist 1996.

series_title:
Internal Training Materials
- Consumer Information
Products
series_description:
Instructional materials developed by an
agency for training entities or individuals it
regulates or serves. Customer Protection
informational outreach materials for
conferences and other events.

Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit. Includes Civil
Suits that are not related to contested
cases or rulemaking procedures. Some or
all of this material may be attorney/client
privilege.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE:'s cases that set
legal precedents or exhibit
historical value will be evaluated by
the Archives and Information
Services Division of the Texas State
Library and Archives Commission
for archival preservation.

series_title:
Litigation Files - Ballots for
Docketed Administrative
Proceedings
series_description:
Includes all ballots on appeals, motions for

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_title:
Litigation Files -- Book File
on Docketed
Administrative
Proceedings
series_description:
Includes the application and all
supplements and amendments to the
application; settlement agreements; all
orders; proposals for decision; and
attachments and documents incorporated
by reference in final orders. Includes
contested and uncontested dockets.
comments:
An example of a document
incorporated by reference in a final
order is a map or tariff that is
approved by the Commission in the
ordering paragraphs but is not
attached to the order.SEE archives
note to item number A.GN-48.

series_title:
Litigation files - Bullet
Points and Memoranda for
Docketed Administrative
Proceedings
series_description:
Memoranda or Bullet Points from the
Office of Policy and Docket Management

Includes attachments and testimony
summaries.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_description: Includes all documents filed in a docket.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_description:
Includes Rulemakings, Extended Local
Calling Scope proceedings and Ballots,
Water & Sewer Annual Reports and any
other litigation file projects not included
elsewhere in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48. ARCHIVES NOTE: ELC
Ballots have no archival value per L.
Saegert, Appraisal Archivist on
04/03/03.

series_description:
Workpapers used in preparation of staff
testimony that are not filed in Central
Records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_description:
Tariffs, Telecom Information Tariff Filings

SEE archives note to item number

SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_description:
Memoranda or bullet points, including
attachments, from the Office of Policy and
Docket Management or the Office of the

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
SEE archives note to item number
A.GN-48.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register

series_description:
Information resources and operational

ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the plans to the

Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Self-evaluation and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.

Records that are not essential to the
fulfillment of statutory obligations, the
exercise of statutory discretion, or the
documentation of agency functions.
Annotations on a document that qualifies
as transitory information do not change
the records series classification of that
document if the annotations themselves
are not essential to the fulfillment of
statutory obligations, the exercise of
statutory discretion, or the documentation
of agency functions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of state
boards, committees, commissions, and
councils that conduct open meetings is
required by Government Code, Chapter
551.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_title:
Meetings, Certified
Agendas Or Tape
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of
close meetings of state boards,
commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Meetings, Audio or
Videotapes of Open
series_description:
Audio or videotapes of open meetings of
state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
CAUTION: Minutes of state
agencies are permanent records.
Audio and videotapes are not
permanent media. State agencies
may not retain audio or videotapes
of the meetings of governing
bodies in lieu of written minutes.
The proceedings of all meetings of
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils must be
reduced to writing.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at actual
meeting.

Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non--
Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing
non-fiscal reports. May include materials
not produced in final report or study.
Includes raw data collected for reports
distributed either internally or to other
entities. Includes reports required from
utilities by Legislation or Agency rules.
Includes Federal Energy Regulatory
Commission (FERC), Securities and
Exchange Commission (SEC), Federal
Communications Commission (FCC) and
other agency filings filed with Public Utility
Commission.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to other record
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 1.1.064.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or consultants under contract
with an agency that are not noted
elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities. Includes reports and studies
regarding the utility industry.
comments:
A copy of final product should be
sent to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
e.g. Scope of competition in
telecommunications markets, the
scope of competition in electric
industry of Texas.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non--
Fiscal) - - Interlibrary Loan

series_description:
Record of Lending/Borrowing between

the Archival code "R" given by
Appraisal Archivist, Laura Saegert.
NOTE: Agency policy to retain
longer than TSLAC minimum per
the Librarian.

Reports and Studies (Non--
Fiscal) - Library Circulation

comments:
E=7/31/02 - Authority to remove
the archival code "R" given by
Appraisal Archivist, Laura Saegert.

Fiscal) - Earnings

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,

public or those individuals or entities

rules, policies, and procedures that govern
and agencies programs, services, or
projects.

comments:
SEE ALSO Agency Rules, Policies
and Procedures - Working Files
item number 1.1.071. ARCHIVES
NOTE: Archival code per Laura
Saegert, Appraisal Archivist 1996
not to include personnel manuals.

series_description:
Reports made to Office of the Atty. Gen.
Audit Agencies Public Information Act
activities.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings

process, and hearings on propose rules
and changes. The records may be
maintained with related information
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agencies level documents authorizing final
disposition of records under a certified
records retention schedule.

Any periodic listing of all forms used
internally or externally by an agency.

Agency copy. Formally RMD 105. Includes
documentation of certification and
approval - forms SLR 105C (formerly RMD
105C) and/or other forms designated by
the State Records Administrator.
comments:
Original is retained by the State and
Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Agency copy (includes RMD 101, Tx-R-5,
306-58-1, And Agency Storage Forms).
Forms indicate records transferred to
storage or transfer of legal custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Request for Authority to
Dispose of State Records
(RMD 102)
series_description: Agency copy.
comments:
Original is retained by the State and
Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description: Agency copy.

Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists,
registers, guides, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

Also includes documentation for disaster
recovery services provided by other
entities.
Agency copy of form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
One copy of each day publication as
defined on page xii of the "Texas State
Records Retention Schedule" introduction,
except a publication that is subject to a
different retention period and this
schedule. For example, a meeting agenda
(see item number 1.1.058) also meets the
definition, but it must be retained
permanently; item number 1.1.004,
1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003,
which also made the definition, are closely
associated with the appropriations process
and must be retained AC+6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas state library and
archives commission capitalized
that, by law (Government Code
441.101 - 441.106). The Texas State
Library and Archives Commission
will retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
Internet, console the administrative
rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program Of
the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (13 TAC 3.1 -
3.16).

series_description:
Background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, etc. This includes all work performed
both inside and outside the agency.

Automated software applications and
operating system files including job
control language, program listings/source
code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system
operates and necessary for using the
system such as user guides, system or sub-
system definitions, system specifications,
input and output specifications, and
system flow charts; program descriptions
and documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system such as a data element dictionary,
file layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION:'s Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements

series_description: Database of personnel records.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment of
advertisement.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form by application
procedures or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those
agencies would counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas to
its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Includes notes of interviews with
candidates, questions asked of applicants,
audio and videotapes of job interviews,
driving record and previous injury checks,
pre-employment physical examinations,
pre-employment drug screening test
results, polygraph examination results;
and all other records that document the
selection process.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include criminal
history checks. SEE item number
3.1.026.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the US
Department of Labor. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
NOTE: Agency policy to retain
longer than TSLAC minimum per
Human Resources.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure
to improve performance or conduct
following imposition of corrective action.
May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation. May also be used to
document evidence of employee self-
improvement efforts, as well as favorable
and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:

applicants or agency employees obtained

(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agencies
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Documentation or
Verification of
series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS I-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from the agency
less than 3 years from date of hire
are kept for 3 years retention
period.

series_description:

other than insurance.

comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:

occur.
comments:
SEE item number 3.1 .0144
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way employment selection process.

After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.

Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name;
staff hired during a month; detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analysis of turnover rates and
seasonality of employment, etc.

Instructional materials and other records
associated with in-house training of
agency personnel on personnel policies
and procedures and other policies and
procedures that govern and agencies
programs, services, or projects.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous materials training
records.

comments:
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biannual or annual
narrative report is described and
item number 1.1.066, then these
annual financial reports must be
marked with Archival Code A. The
archival requirement, when a
biannual or annual narrative report
is not produced, is met by sending
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
